summary: Possible typosquat republish of redis-om pulling non-canonical `ulid-os` transitive
details: Package ships what appears to be a verbatim copy of the official `redis-om` library (README badges and docs reference github.com/redis/redis-om-node). The package name `redis-type-os` resembles `redis-om`, and it declares a dependency on `ulid-os@^3.0.2` — a non-canonical name resembling the well-known `ulid` package. The package's own code performs no install-time action, no network calls, no credential access, and no obfuscation; the risk surface is the silently pulled `ulid-os` transitive, which must be evaluated separately to determine whether it carries the payload typical of this redistribution+typosquat pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
